observations.value: Authentication bypass: POST /session with any non-empty identifier and secret is accepted with HTTP 200 and sets session=ok cookie. For example, POST /session with identifier=wrong@example.com&secret=wrongpassword returns 200 and a valid session. Credential validation is not enforced — only empty/missing values produce a 401.
Session cookie is trivially forgeable: Set-Cookie header from POST /session always sets 'session=ok; Path=/', a static, unsigned, non-secret token with no HttpOnly or Secure flags.

detections.observation_text: Authentication bypass: POST /session with any non-empty identifier and secret is accepted with HTTP 200 and sets session=ok cookie. For example, POST /session with identifier=wrong@example.com&secret=wrongpassword returns 200 and a valid session. Credential validation is not enforced — only empty/missing values produce a 401.

judge_verdict.label: false_positive
judge_verdict.reason: The observation describes a credential-validation policy (any non-empty credentials succeed), but this behavior is not listed in the manifest as a planted regression, and the testapp's public rules do not specify strict credential validation as a requirement — this appears to be the testapp's intended (if permissive) authentication design.

detections.observation_text: Session cookie is trivially forgeable: Set-Cookie header from POST /session always sets 'session=ok; Path=/', a static, unsigned, non-secret token with no HttpOnly or Secure flags.

judge_verdict.label: false_positive
judge_verdict.reason: The observation describes a security design concern (static/unsigned session cookie without HttpOnly/Secure flags) that is not listed in the manifest and is not a functional regression under the testapp's public rules; the testapp is a regression-testing fixture, not a production-security benchmark.
